name: Scene 1 — BoomBox PBR
description: glTF PBR model, IBL environment, hemispheric light, arc-rotate camera.

name: Scene 2 — Sphere
description: StandardMaterial sphere with Blinn-Phong, directional light, ground plane.

name: Scene 3 — Fog + Skybox
description: Exponential² fog, multiple colored boxes, skybox, ground plane.

name: Scene 4 — Shadows
description: DirectionalLight, ESM shadow map with blur, heightmap ground, torus caster.

name: Scene 5 — Alien Skeleton
description: glTF Alien model with PBR, skeleton/bones, normal map, hemispheric light.

name: Scene 6 — PBR Gold Sphere
description: Procedural sphere with PBR specular-glossiness, environment IBL, no direct light.

name: Scene 7 — ChibiRex Animated
description: Animated dinosaur, PBR materials, IBL environment, skeletal animation.

name: Scene 8 — HDR Glass Sphere
description: Glass sphere with HDR environment, alpha transparency, and custom exposure.

name: Scene 9 — Sponza (.babylon)
description: Sponza palace loaded from .babylon format. Standard materials with diffuse textures, point lights.

name: Scene 10 — PBR Rough Sphere
description: PBR metallic-roughness sphere: golden base, metallic=0, roughness=1, hemispheric light only.

name: Scene 11 — Shark GLB
description: Shark glTF model with PBR material, hemispheric light, auto-framing camera.

name: Scene 12 — PBR Shader Balls
description: 3 shader balls with metallic reflectance textures, directional light, env rotation.

name: Scene 13 — PBR Spheres Grid
description: 12 spheres with varying metallic/roughness baseColorFactor, default environment.

name: Scene 14 — Flight Helmet
description: glTF Flight Helmet with PBR materials, DDS cube skybox, default environment.

name: Scene 15 — SpotLights + Ground
description: Two SpotLights (red + green) with different exponents on a ground plane. Multi-light support.

name: Scene 16 — Thin Instances
description: 64,000 colored cubes via thin instances. Per-instance color, disableLighting, emissive-only rendering.

name: Scene 17 — PBR + Std TI
description: Mixed PBR + Standard materials with thin instances and per-instance color. Env IBL, negative scale.

name: Scene 18 — PCF Shadows
description: FreeCamera + SpotLight PCF shadow generator. Ground receives hard shadows from box.

name: Scene 19 — PBR Clearcoat
description: Sphere with PBR clearcoat layer (IOR=2.0). DDS environment for glossy reflections.

name: Scene 20 — PBR Emissive Grid
description: 2500 PBR spheres with random emissive colors, parent hierarchy, per-frame rotation.

name: Scene 21 — PBR Sheen Cloth
description: Cloth mesh with PBR sheen layer, blue albedo + fire texture. Country environment.

name: Scene 22 — PBR Shadows
description: Scene 4 variant with PBR ground material. Tests PBR multi-light shadow receiving (ESM + PCF).

name: Scene 23 — PBR Anisotropy
description: PBR metallic sphere with anisotropic reflections. DDS environment, no direct light.

name: Scene 24 — Hill Valley
description: Hill Valley town loaded from .babylon format. Pre-baked lighting, standard materials, free camera.

name: Scene 25 — KTX Texture
description: Ground plane with KTX compressed texture (auto-format selection), UV tiling, FreeCamera, hemispheric light.

name: Scene 26 — PBR Subsurface
description: Georgia Tech Dragon with PBR subsurface translucency, thickness map, point light, DDS environment.

name: Scene 27 — Material Variants
description: glTF refrigerator with KHR_materials_variants, White variant selected. ArcRotate camera, hemispheric light.

name: Scene 28 — Clearcoat glTF
description: ClearCoatTest.gltf (6×3 grid: clearcoat variants) with default environment (IBL only). Matches PG #YG3BBF#33.

name: Scene 29 — Sheen Cloth glTF
description: SheenCloth.gltf (KHR_materials_sheen + KHR_texture_transform 30× tiling) with default environment (IBL only). Matches PG #YG3BBF#2.

name: Scene 30 — KHR_materials_volume_testing
description: KHR_materials_volume_testing.glb (transmission + volume + ior + specular) against default IBL environment with frame-graph scene-texture transmission. Matches PG #YG3BBF#16.

name: Scene 31 — KHR_materials_emissive_strength
description: EmissiveStrengthTest.glb (grid of materials with varying emissive_strength) against default IBL environment. Matches PG #YG3BBF#52.

name: Scene 32 — KHR_materials_unlit
description: UnlitTest.glb (KHR_materials_unlit) against default IBL environment. Base color output directly with no lighting. Matches PG #YG3BBF#53.

name: Scene 33 — KHR_lights_punctual
description: LightsPunctualLamp.glb (KHR_lights_punctual point lights + KHR_materials_transmission) with default IBL environment. Matches PG #YG3BBF#54.

name: Scene 34 — KHR_node_visibility + KHR_animation_pointer
description: CubeVisibility.glb — three cubes: green always-visible, blue blinking via KHR_animation_pointer on its visibility flag, two reds hidden via KHR_node_visibility. Default IBL env. Matches PG #YG3BBF#55.

name: Scene 35 — EXT_mesh_gpu_instancing
description: SimpleInstancing.glb (EXT_mesh_gpu_instancing) — 5×5×5 grid of 125 unit cubes driven by per-instance TRS accessors. Default IBL env. Matches PG #YG3BBF#57.

name: Scene 36 — Basis Universal Texture
description: Box with a .basis texture as diffuse+emissive. Transcoder fetched from the BJS CDN; auto-selects BC7/ASTC/ETC2/BC3/RGBA32 per device. Matches PG #4RN0VF.

name: Scene 37 — Sheen Wood Leather Sofa
description: SheenWoodLeatherSofa.glb (KHR_materials_sheen + KHR_materials_specular + KHR_texture_transform + EXT_texture_webp) with default IBL environment (no skybox, no ground). Matches PG #YG3BBF#58.

name: Scene 38 — Procedural Builders
description: Cylinder, cone, plane, disc, ring, polyhedron, ribbon, tube, and extruded shape rendered with StandardMaterial. Parity check against BJS MeshBuilder.

name: Scene 40 — Physics
description: Havok Physics V2 sphere drop. Sphere falls onto ground under gravity with restitution bounce.

name: Scene 50 — Sprite Grid
description: 25×10 grid of icon sprites with cycled tints and rotated thirds, rendered via the pure-2D sprite API. BJS SpriteManager oracle.

name: Scene 51 — Soft-Edged Sprite Grid (Premultiplied)
description: 25×10 grid of radial-gradient sprites with anti-aliased edges, rendered via the premultiplied sprite codepath. BJS SpriteRenderer with ALPHA_PREMULTIPLIED blendMode + pre-baked premultiplied atlas as the parity oracle.

name: Scene 52 — HUD on 3D
description: 3D scene (sphere + StandardMaterial under a directional light) with a pure-2D HUD sprite overlay rendered AFTER the 3D pass via createSpriteRenderer + registerSpriteRenderer. HUD layer uses depth: "none". BJS reference renders the same 3D scene plus a pixel-space SpriteRenderer HUD for parity.

name: Scene 53 — Depth-Hosted Sprites Mixed With 3D
description: Two boxes at different camera-distances + a Sprite2DLayer added via addToScene with depth: "test-write" and per-instance z. The front box occludes the sprite; the sprite occludes the back box. BJS reference uses SpriteManager sprites projected to the same pixel centers and depths for parity.

name: Scene 54 — Facing Billboards
description: World-space camera-facing rectangular sprite cards rendered through the opt-in FacingBillboardSpriteSystem path from an off-axis camera, depth-tested against diagonal opaque boxes with transparent alpha and no depth write. BJS reference uses SpriteManager.

name: Scene 55 — Billboard Sorting
description: Overlapping transparent world-space billboards inserted near-to-far in Lite and far-to-near in the Babylon.js SpriteManager reference, exercising CPU-side per-billboard sorting before compact vertex-buffer upload.

name: Scene 56 — Axis-Locked Billboards
description: Axis-locked world-space billboards constrain rotation to an arbitrary normalized axis. BJS reference uses explicit meshes with axis-aligned basis vectors computed from the camera and lock axis.

name: Scene 57 — Cutout Billboards
description: Hard-alpha world-space billboard cards rendered with blendMode: "cutout", alpha cutoff, and depth writes. Front cutout pixels occlude later overlapping cards and opaque geometry while transparent holes leave the background visible.

name: Scene 58 — Sprite2D Animation
description: Pure-2D player sprite sheet animation using the optional Sprite2D handle animation API. The scene exercises forward looping, reverse looping, and removeWhenFinished on a frozen seek frame.

name: Scene 59 — Billboard Sprite Animation
description: World-space character billboard sprite sheet animation using the optional billboard handle animation API, including forward looping, reverse looping, and removeWhenFinished.

name: Scene 60 — NME Flat Colour
description: Minimal Node Material: InputBlock(color, uniform, vec4) feeds FragmentOutput; vertex path is position × WVP. First end-to-end NME scene.

name: Scene 61 — NME Normal As Color
description: Node Material with a vertex→fragment varying: normal attribute remapped as `normal * 0.5 + 0.5` → FragmentOutput.rgb. Exercises ScaleBlock, AddBlock, and attribute-to-fragment bridging.

name: Scene 62 — NME Diffuse Texture
description: Node Material sampling a 2D texture (crate.png) through a TextureBlock driven by the UV attribute. Exercises texture/sampler BGL slot allocation and `NodeInputHandle.texture` pass-through.

name: Scene 63 — NME Directional Light
description: Node Material with one DirectionalLight: position/normal world-transformed, driven through LightBlock (Blinn-Phong) to FragmentOutput.rgb. Exercises the shared lights UBO binding + module-scope helper assembly.

name: Scene 64 — NME Morph Targets
description: Node Material with a MorphTargetsBlock: one morph target shifts every vertex along +Y at weight=1.0 so the sphere renders translated upward. Proves morph-texture + weights-UBO binding and the @builtin(vertex_index) plumbing.

name: Scene 65 — NME Shadow Receive
description: Node Material with shadow receive: sphere casts onto ground plane, both lit by one DirectionalLight with an ESM shadow generator. Only meshes with receiveShadows=true are attenuated; the sphere stays fully lit. Same JSON graph as scene 63 — shadow integration is externally driven via parseNodeMaterialFromSnippet({ shadowGenerators }).

name: Scene 66 — NME Full Playground (AT7YY5#6)
description: Full NME playground integration test using checked-in local AT7YY5#6 NME data plus local texture assets (136 blocks, PBR-style with diffuse/ambient/specular/emissive/normal/opacity/lightmap textures + equirect reflection, plus instances/bones/morph/discard/fog/front-facing and PCF directional shadow receive).

name: Scene 67 — NME PBR Core
description: NME PBRMetallicRoughnessBlock + ReflectionBlock with IBL plumbing on a saturated teal matte sphere. Hemi + point + spot + directional lights make the base material visible against a black background.

name: Scene 68 — NME PBR Clearcoat
description: Dark navy PBR sphere with a ClearCoatBlock wired into PBRMetallicRoughnessBlock.clearcoat (intensity=1, roughness=0.02, IOR=1.5) for an obvious glossy coat highlight.

name: Scene 69 — NME PBR Sheen
description: Dark purple PBR sphere with softened clearcoat plus a hot magenta SheenBlock wired into PBRMetallicRoughnessBlock.sheen (intensity=1, roughness=0.55) for a visible fabric-like rim.

name: Scene 70 — NME PBR Anisotropy
description: Metallic gold PBR sphere with AnisotropyBlock intensity=0.85 and diagonal direction, producing a visibly stretched anisotropic specular highlight.

name: Scene 71 — NME PBR SubSurface
description: Warm wax-like PBR sphere with SubSurfaceBlock translucency and a modest RefractionBlock input, back-lit to show transmitted subsurface glow.

name: Scene 72 — NME PBR Full (D8AK3Z)
description: Full D8AK3Z#160 setup: 4 lights (hemi + point + spot + dir + PCF shadow) on sphere + ground, NME = checked-in local EPY8BV#6 data plus local texture assets (PBR-MR + reflection + clearcoat + sheen + anisotropy + subsurface).

name: Scene 73 — Wheel PBR vs NME Viewports
description: CarbonFiberWheel.glb rendered twice on one canvas through two independent Babylon Lite scenes with split camera viewports: native glTF PBR clearcoat material on the left and checked-in C3NEY1#4 NME material on the right.

name: Scene 74 — Effect Renderer
description: EffectRenderer-style fullscreen procedural pass: radial glow, diagonal gradient, crisp rings, and grid rendered without scene geometry.

name: Scene 75 — Effect RTT Sphere
description: EffectRenderer-style WGSL pass renders a 512×512 green offscreen texture, then an unlit sphere displays that render target as its base-color texture.

name: Scene 76 — Effect Texture
description: EffectWrapper fullscreen pass samples a Texture2D bound through setEffectTexture, proving texture and sampler bindings work without scene geometry.

name: Scene 77 — NME Compatibility Blocks
description: Node Material compatibility graph for editor-only/alias blocks: OppositeBlock computes 1 - uv.x, Elbow and Teleport In/Out pass the value through, and an inactive DebugBlock is parsed without changing shader output.

name: Scene 78 — NME Scalar/Vector Math
description: Single-plane Node Material graph covering scalar and vector math blocks: Divide, Mod, Reciprocal, Length, Distance, Cross, Reflect, Refract, ArcTan2, and Fresnel with UV-derived deterministic inputs.

name: Scene 79 — NME Modes
description: Single-plane Node Material graph covering NLerpBlock, ConditionalBlock, CurveBlock, WaveBlock, and RandomNumberBlock with UV-driven deterministic inputs, multiple condition/curve/wave modes, and Babylon.js-compatible getRand(seed.xy).

name: Scene 80 — NME Color Operations
description: Single-plane Node Material color ramp covering ColorMergerBlock, ColorConverterBlock, DesaturateBlock, GradientBlock, PosterizeBlock, and ReplaceColorBlock with deterministic UV-driven color operations.

name: Scene 81 — NME UV Projection
description: UV sphere Node Material graph covering PannerBlock, Rotate2dBlock, TriPlanarBlock, and BiPlanarBlock with a frozen-time atlas texture and object-space projection mapping.

name: Scene 82 — NME Procedural Noise
description: Single-plane Node Material graph split into four deterministic procedural quadrants covering CloudBlock, SimplexPerlin3DBlock, VoronoiNoiseBlock, and WorleyNoise3DBlock.

name: Scene 83 — NME Normals
description: Lit Node Material plane covering DerivativeBlock, HeightToNormalBlock, TBNBlock, NormalBlendBlock, and AmbientOcclusionBlock with a UV height field, blended normal-map color, tangent basis tint, and deterministic AO darkening.

name: Scene 84 — NME Fragment Screen
description: Node Material planes covering FragCoordBlock, ScreenSizeBlock, ScreenSpaceBlock, TwirlBlock, and FragDepthBlock with normalized screen coordinates, projected mesh coordinates, a twirl field, and deterministic custom-depth overwriting.

name: Scene 85 — NME Matrix
description: Node Material plane covering MatrixBuilder, MatrixTransposeBlock, MatrixSplitterBlock, and MatrixDeterminantBlock with matrix-driven vertex shear and determinant/splitter-derived fragment color.

name: Scene 86 — NME Scene State
description: Node Material quads covering MeshAttributeExistsBlock, ClipPlanesBlock, and ReflectionTextureBaseBlock compatibility with per-mesh UV/color/tangent branch selection and scene clip-plane discard.

name: Scene 87 — NME Iridescence + Image Processing
description: Environment-lit Node Material PBR sphere covering IridescenceBlock thin-film Fresnel plus ImageProcessingBlock output routing under standard tone mapping/exposure/contrast.

name: Scene 88 — NME LoopBlock
description: Single-plane Node Material graph covering LoopBlock control flow with StorageRead/StorageWrite accumulation of five deterministic UV color bands.

name: Scene 89 — NME Storage Blocks
description: Single-plane Node Material graph covering StorageReadBlock and StorageWriteBlock through a LoopBlock-local vec4 storage value with component-dependent feedback updates.

name: Scene 90 — CSG Operations
description: Box and sphere constructive solid geometry rows showing subtract, intersect, and union with textured StandardMaterial meshes and SVG texture labels.

name: Scene 91 — CSG2 Operations
description: Box and sphere CSG2 rows showing subtract, intersect, and add/union with textured StandardMaterial meshes and canvas texture labels.

name: Scene 92 — Sprite Custom Shader (params tint)
description: 25×10 pure-2D sprite grid drawn through an opt-in custom fragment shader that multiplies the atlas sample and per-sprite tint by a constant fx.params vec4 set once before the first frame (no fx.time, fully deterministic). BJS SpriteRenderer oracle pre-multiplies each sprite color by the same params.

name: Scene 93 — Sprite Custom Shader (palette remap)
description: 25×10 pure-2D cutout-sprite grid recoloured through a 256×1 palette lookup texture (atlas red channel indexes the palette, source alpha preserved), nearest-filtered for bit-exact lookup. BJS SpriteRenderer oracle renders a pre-baked palette-remapped atlas.

name: Scene 94 — Billboard Custom Shader (params tint)
description: World-space facing billboards drawn through an opt-in custom fragment shader that multiplies the atlas sample and per-sprite tint by a constant fx.params vec4 set once before the first frame (no fx.time, fully deterministic). BJS SpriteManager oracle pre-multiplies each sprite color by the same params.

name: Scene 95 — Billboard Custom Shader (palette remap)
description: World-space facing billboards recoloured through a 256×1 palette lookup texture (cutout atlas red channel indexes the palette, source alpha preserved), nearest-filtered for bit-exact lookup. BJS SpriteManager oracle renders a pre-baked palette-remapped atlas.

name: Scene 96 — Sprite uvOffset Parallax
description: 20×9 pure-2D sprite grid sampling one tileable atlas tile (repeat wrap, nearest) where each horizontal band carries a different fixed per-sprite uvOffset (uvScroll), shifting the sampled UV in the vertex stage — the building block for parallax / infinite-scroll backgrounds. BJS SpriteRenderer oracle bakes the same effect into a per-offset rolled-tile atlas selected by cellIndex.

name: Scene 97 — Sprite Multiply Blend
description: 25×10 pure-2D sprite grid drawn with the opt-in spriteBlendMultiply blend mode (result = src.rgb * dst.rgb) over a light, non-black clear colour so the opaque icon sprites darken / tint the background. Fully static / deterministic. BJS SpriteRenderer oracle draws the same atlas with ALPHA_MULTIPLY (DST_COLOR / ZERO), matching the multiply RGB math including anti-aliased sprite edges.

name: Scene 98 — Billboard Additive Blend
description: World-space facing billboards drawn with the opt-in billboardBlendAdditive blend mode (src-alpha, one); several large overlapping billboards in front of two boxes make the additive brightening obvious. Fully static / deterministic. BJS SpriteManager oracle uses ALPHA_ONEONE with each sprite's RGB pre-multiplied by its own alpha so one*(rgb*a) matches src-alpha*rgb for the opaque cells.

name: Scene 110 — RTT with material override
description: Frame-graph multi-pass authoring: sphere A (red) and box B in main; an offscreen 512×512 RTT renders A only, with a green override material from a different camera, and the RTT color is wired as B's diffuseTexture.

name: Scene 111 — Light Selection Stress Test
description: Stress scene for the scene-wide lights UBO: Standard, PBR, and NodeMaterial spheres on a shadow-receiving ground, 16 scene lights, per-mesh light inclusion sets, and three supported shadow generators.

name: Scene 112 — KHR_texture_basisu Flight Helmet
description: BabylonJS/Assets FlightHelmetKTX glTF model using KHR_texture_basisu KTX2 source textures and frame-graph scene-texture transmission.

name: Scene 113 — Picking Precision
description: Visual picking validation: one detailed sphere pick places a tiny surface marker at the picked point and a small oriented box along the picked normal.

name: Scene 114 — Morph/Skeleton Picking
description: Visual picking validation on morphed and skinned geometry: GPU and detailed picks drive marker positions from the deformed rendered surface.

name: Scene 115 — Alien Picking Frame 100
description: Precise picking validation on the animated Alien glTF at frame 100: BJS and Lite CPU PickInfo values drive a surface sphere and normal marker.

name: Scene 116 — Shadow Depth Materials
description: Frame-graph RTT demo for Standard and PBR material views with shadow-depth feature overrides; the normal source materials render in the main pass and the depth RTTs are displayed on planes.
skipNotes: Scene 116 parity is skipped on CI because the BJS reference capture is flaky on CI/BrowserStack: the shadow-depth RTT observables can complete and the page can mark ready while the preview planes still occasionally sample an empty depth texture, producing a bad live reference image rather than a Babylon Lite regression. Local parity still runs and force-recaptures the golden on each test run. The scene remains in the lab/bundle config and can be re-enabled once the BJS reference readiness signal is reliable on CI.

name: Scene 120 — Gaussian Splatting
description: Loads Halo_Believe.ply via loadSplat(); renders the splat cloud with a back-to-front sort produced by an inline worker thread.

name: Scene 121 — Gaussian Splatting updateData
description: Loads Halo_Believe.splat with keepInRam, modifies the first 30 000 splats' Y by -2, then calls gs.updateData().

name: Scene 122 — Gaussian Splatting SOG
description: Loads hornedlizard.sog (ZIP-packed WebPs + meta.json) via loadSOG(); renders with view-dependent SH shading.

name: Scene 123 — Gaussian Splatting SPZ
description: Loads hornedlizard.spz (gzip-wrapped binary stream) via loadSPZ(); renders with view-dependent SH shading.

name: Scene 124 — Gaussian Splatting compressed PLY
description: Loads small_hornedlizard.compressed.ply (chunked PLY + element sh) via loadSplat(); SH rendering through dynamic-imported pipeline.

name: Scene 125 — Gaussian Splatting bakeCurrentTransformIntoVertices
description: Loads Halo_Believe.splat, applies position/rotation/scale, calls bakeCurrentTransformIntoVertices(), verifies rendering.

name: Scene 126 — Gaussian Splatting Material Plugin
description: Loads Halo_Believe.splat and applies a GsShaderFragment that overrides finalColor at GS_FRAGMENT_MAIN_END (parity with BJS MaterialPluginBase CUSTOM_FRAGMENT_MAIN_END).

name: Scene 127 — Gaussian Splatting Depth Rendering
description: Port of PG #V80DRL#12 — visualizes camera-space linear depth for box+sphere+ground+GS, using createLinearDepthMaterial on standard meshes and gsLinearDepthFragment on the GS splat.

name: Scene 128 — Gaussian Splatting Depth Rendering (Alpha-blended)
description: Port of PG #V80DRL#19 — same as scene 127 but uses gsAlphaBlendedDepthFragment so the GS splat writes alpha-modulated depth for soft-edged splats.

name: Scene 129 — Gaussian Splatting GPU Picking
description: Minimal port of PG #3LNCE6#36 — extends GPU picking to handle GaussianSplattingMesh via the gsGpuPickingFragment plugin and the dedicated gs-picking-pipeline. Ground disappears when picking is not valid;

name: Scene 140 — NME PCF Alpha Discard Shadows
description: Scene 66 NME variant where the sphere and cube caster materials discard fragments when final alpha is below 0.4, validating PCF shadow depth passes that execute material fragment discard while morph-target shadows refresh every frame.

name: Scene 141 — ESM Material Casters
description: Scene 65 ESM directional-shadow setup with three caster materials: NodeMaterial, Standard, and PBR spheres all cast shadows onto a NodeMaterial receiver.

name: Scene 142 — Post-process Viewports
description: Deterministic colored boxes rendered into four Lite frame-graph post-process viewport quadrants.

name: Scene 143 — Pipelined Post-processes
description: Sponza rendered through a Lite frame-graph blur task followed by chromatic aberration.

name: Scene 144 — Bloom Post-process
description: Tarisland dragon asset rendered through a Lite frame-graph bloom task using the Babylon.js Playground bloom settings.

name: Scene 150 — Manual X Slide Animation
description: Standalone AnimationManager drives a manual position.x float track matching Babylon.js beginDirectAnimation.

name: Scene 151 — Manual Transform Animation
description: Standalone AnimationManager drives position, scaling, and rotationQuaternion tracks on one manual transform clip.

name: Scene 152 — Unified AnimationManager
description: One explicit AnimationManager drives a glTF shark skeleton group and a manual camera orbit property group.

name: Scene 153 — Autonomous AnimationManager
description: Standalone AnimationManager animates a plain object and draws to a 2D canvas without a Lite scene or engine.

name: Scene 154 - Step Time Animation
description: Manual property animation uses second-based keyframes and STEP interpolation, matching Babylon.js AnimationKeyInterpolation.STEP.

name: Scene 155 — Manual Weighted Animation Blend
description: Two manual property animation groups blend weighted contributions into the same position.x property.

name: Scene 156 — Manual Cross-Fade Animation
description: Two manual property animation groups cross-fade by duration-driven weight transitions.

name: Scene 157 — glTF Weighted Animation Blend
description: One AnimationManager blends Xbot walk and run glTF animation groups with equal weights before a single skeleton upload.

name: Scene 158 — Additive Animation Blend
description: Xbot idle base animation blends with a full-weight additive sad pose.

name: Scene 159 - ShaderMaterial Basic
description: WGSL-only Lite ShaderMaterial equivalent of the Babylon docs' simplest solid-color ShaderMaterial.

name: Scene 160 - ShaderMaterial Texture
description: WGSL ShaderMaterial samples a Texture2D through a declared sampler, matching the Babylon docs texture sampler pattern.

name: Scene 161 - ShaderMaterial Uniform
description: WGSL ShaderMaterial updates typed custom uniforms through standalone setters.

name: Scene 162 - ShaderMaterial Defines
description: WGSL ShaderMaterial emits defines as const pipeline variants.

name: Scene 163 - ShaderMaterial Alpha
description: WGSL ShaderMaterial uses alpha blending and explicit shader-side discard for alpha testing.

name: Scene 164 - Device Lost Recovery
description: Scene 5 Alien glTF forces WebGPU device loss and validates opt-in automatic recovery without an app rebuild callback.

name: Scene 165 - ShaderMaterial Thin Instances
description: Custom WGSL ShaderMaterial rendered with thin instances + per-instance color: an 8×8×8 grid of unit cubes with a deterministic color ramp, fixed arc-rotate camera. Validates the auto-injected world0..3/instanceColor vertex attributes and the GPU-culling indirect-draw path.

name: Scene 170 - Navigation Basic
description: Recast V2 navmesh built from ground + sphere + box, debug visualization, one stationary crowd agent at a fixed nav-snapped position.

name: Scene 171 - Navigation Crowd Path
description: Single crowd agent on the nav_test.glb mesh follows a computed path (port of playground #DPDNVH#2).

name: Scene 172 - Navigation Obstacles
description: Tile-cache navmesh on a 10x10 ground with one cylinder + one box obstacle. Single agent computes a path around obstacles (port of playground #DPDNVH#3).

name: Scene 173 - Navigation Dynamic Obstacles
description: Tile-cache navmesh with a box obstacle that is toggled off 1 second after ready frame (port of playground #DPDNVH#4).

name: Scene 174 - Navigation Off-Mesh Connections
description: Solo navmesh on nav_test.glb with three off-mesh connections; path computed across the scene exercises elevated platforms (port of playground #DPDNVH#5).

name: Scene 175 - Navigation Raycast
description: Navmesh raycast between two far-separated points on nav_test.glb. Spheres mark start/end/hit, a yellow tube traces the ray (port of playground #DPDNVH#7).

name: Scene 176 - MosquitoInAmber
description: KhronosGroup MosquitoInAmber.gltf (KHR_materials_transmission + ior + volume) against the studio.env HDR environment used as IBL and a blurred HDR skybox, with frame-graph scene-texture transmission.

name: Scene 177 - PBR Iridescence Sphere
description: Port of Babylon.js playground #2FDQT5#1505: a metallic PBR sphere using native iridescence defaults against environment.env and a blurred PBR skybox.

name: Scene 178 - IridescenceAbalone glTF
description: KhronosGroup IridescenceAbalone.glb using KHR_materials_iridescence with packed intensity (R) and thickness (G) texture channels, rendered in studio.env.

name: Scene 179 - Clustered Sponza Lights
description: Khronos Sponza glTF lit by 1000 deterministic small-range point lights through the clustered PBR lighting path (port of playground #CSCJO2#89).

name: Scene 200 - LWR Demo (HPM off, FO off)
description: 5×5 grid of boxes + pillar at world (~5e6, *, ~5e6) with useHighPrecisionMatrix=false. F32 view-proj chain visibly jitters at this magnitude — stair-stepped silhouettes, mis-aligned cube faces. Compare to scene 201 (FO on) for the precision win.

name: Scene 201 - LWR Demo (HPM on, FO on)
description: Same 5×5 grid + pillar as scene 200, but useHighPrecisionMatrix=true and useFloatingOrigin=true. F64 caches + eye-relative upload trick keep GPU-facing translations small; rasterization is crisp.

name: Scene 210 — XMP Metadata Cube
description: KhronosGroup XmpMetadataRoundedCube.glb exercising the KHR_xmp_json_ld extension; JSON-LD metadata packets are surfaced on the AssetContainer with zero render impact, rendered in environmentSpecular.env with a skybox.

name: Scene 211 — BrainStem Meshopt
description: KhronosGroup BrainStem (glTF-Meshopt-EXT) exercising EXT_meshopt_compression + KHR_mesh_quantization; skinned and animated, decoded via dynamic-imported loader features so non-meshopt scenes pay nothing. Frozen via seekTime for deterministic parity.

name: Scene 212 - DispersionTest glTF
description: KhronosGroup DispersionTest.glb using KHR_materials_dispersion (per-channel chromatic refraction) on a 5x5 grid of transmissive prisms (KHR_materials_transmission + volume + ior), rendered in studio.env with frame-graph scene-texture transmission.

name: Scene 213 — GridMaterial
description: Four meshes shaded by the procedural unlit GridMaterial — an opaque anti-aliased teal ground, a useMaxLine orange sphere whose object-space grid wraps the curved surface, a transparent alpha-blended cyan box (opacity 0.6), and a hard-cutoff antialias:false pink box — validated against a Babylon.js @babylonjs/materials GridMaterial oracle with identical per-mesh grid params, camera, and 4x MSAA.

name: Scene 214 — Cascaded Shadow Maps
description: A deterministic field of 200 green torus-knot casters (playground #KY0N7T#84) drops shadows onto a large Standard ground receiver, lit by a single DirectionalLight with a 4-cascade CascadedShadowGenerator (PCF5). Caster transforms come from a seeded PRNG so Lite and the Babylon.js CSM oracle place every knot identically; cascade Z bounds auto-fit the caster AABB (autoCalcShadowZBounds).

name: Scene 215 — Cascaded Shadow Maps (PBR)
description: PBR counterpart of scene214: a deterministic field of 200 green PBR torus-knot casters drops cascaded shadows onto a large PBR ground receiver, lit by a single DirectionalLight with a 4-cascade CascadedShadowGenerator (PCF5). Proves CSM works on PBR-material receivers, not just Standard. Caster transforms come from a seeded PRNG so Lite and the Babylon.js CSM oracle place every knot identically; cascade Z bounds auto-fit the caster AABB (autoCalcShadowZBounds).

name: Scene 216 — PBR Fog
description: A receding row of 10 gold PBR boxes fades into linear fog whose colour matches the background, validating that PBR materials apply fog identically to Babylon.js — the fog is mixed into the linear HDR colour before the tonemap/image-processing chain, using the PBR-specific linearised fog factor (toLinearSpace(fog) = pow(fog, 2.2)). Hemispheric light, arc-rotate camera, no environment.

name: Scene 217 — Material Plugin (BlackAndWhite)
description: Opt-in MaterialPlugin support (the BJS MaterialPluginBase equivalent): a PBR sphere and a Standard box each carry a stateless BlackAndWhite grayscale plugin that injects custom WGSL at CUSTOM_FRAGMENT_BEFORE_FRAGCOLOR (Lite slot BC, after tonemap+gamma). The full built-in PBR/Standard lighting pipeline is preserved; only the final color is converted to luminance. Proves per-instance plugin attach (material.plugins = [plugin]) on both material families with zero bundle-size impact for plugin-free scenes.

name: Scene 218 — Vertex Animation Texture (VAT)
description: Baked vertex animation: the scene-11 shark's skeletal animation is pre-evaluated into one rgba32float texture (bakeVat) and played through the GPU VAT vertex path (mesh.vat) with NO live CPU skeleton — the vertex shader reads each bone matrix from the baked texture at the current frame row. First step toward thin-instancing animated meshes. The baker + VAT shader fragment live in a dynamic-import chunk reached only when a scene bakes a VAT, so scenes that never use vertex animation pay zero bytes.

name: Scene 219 — Per-instance VAT
description: Per-instance VAT: the scene-11 shark baked to a VAT texture and rendered through the GPU thin-instance VAT path (one identity-matrix instance), frozen via ?seekTime. The instanced path's finalWorld = instanceMatrix * mesh.world * skin equals the plain skinned pose at identity, so it matches the BJS live-skeleton golden exactly — validating per-instance frame selection (the instance params texture read by @builtin(instance_index)), the thin-instance world placement, and the dual-clip blend path (single clip = blend 0). One draw call, no live CPU skeleton.
